perturbation.original_text: "Business Associate" means the Kallo Authorized Agent who could be an individual, organization, or company that desires to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products of Kallo Inc., to the end user (customer) market. The Business Associate as it will be defined in this agreement sells the Kallo Mobile care product suite only. Kallo will be fully responsible for the training and implementation of the client
perturbation.changed_text: "Business Associate" means the Kallo Authorized Agent who could be an individual, organization, or company that desires to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products of Kallo Inc., to the end user (customer) market. The Business Associate as it will be defined in this agreement markets the Kallo Mobile care product suite only. Agent will be fully responsible for the training and implementation of the client
perturbation.explanation: Here, the term **'Business Associate'** is being redefined. The original text stated that the 'Business Associate' *sells* the product and Kallo is responsible for training. The changed text says that Business Associate *markets* the product instead of sells, and that Agent will be fully responsible for the training and implementation instead of Kallo. This contradicts the original definition by altering the nature of their engagement from selling to marketing and who will be responsible for training.
perturbation.contradicted_text: following the sale directly by Kallo Inc., or through it's wholly owned Canadian subsidiary, Kallo Technologies Inc., "KALLO" means Kallo Inc. and/or its wholly owned subsidiary Kallo Technologies Inc., "Customer" means the end user of the Kallo Mobile care suite of Products that enters into the Kallo Mobile care Suite of Products End User License Agreement (EULA). "Schedule A" means the Kallo supplied executable pricing proposal form which must accompany any EULA for the Kallo Mobile care suite of Products in order to be accepted by Kallo. "Kallo Mobile care suite of Products" means Kallo products as specified in Schedule A.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines the 'Business Associate' role and responsibilities, shifting from 'selling' to 'marketing' and changing who is responsible for training. However, the contradicted text focuses on defining other terms like 'Kallo', 'Customer', 'Schedule A', and 'Kallo Mobile care suite of Products'. There isn't a direct contradiction because the contradicted section doesn't define the responsibilities, of Kallo or Business associate. Therefore, the change in definition doesn't create a direct conflict with the meaning or obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Certification is mandatory for Agent to become certified to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products licensed under this agreement and to benefit from commissions allocated to Agent as outlined in Schedule A of this agreement. Non-compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will automatically void this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Certification is not mandatory for Agent to become certified to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products licensed under this agreement and to benefit from commissions allocated to Agent as outlined in Schedule A of this agreement. Non-compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will automatically void this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says Certification *is mandatory* for the Agent in order to proceed with certain actions that benefit the agent within the contract. The modified text indicates that Certification *is not mandatory.* If there is no certification mandated, the Agent may not perform certain roles, as described in Schedule A, and still cause automatic voidance of the agreement. This introduces an in-text contradiction as the agreement is automatically voided if the agent does not have certification but it also states that certification is not mandatory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agent is required to pay a Training program fee of $10,000.00 for the Kallo Mobile care suite of Products on or before the date of execution of this agreement for the Authorization of Agent under this agreement by Kallo. Agent shall pay $1000/- per day for any additional days of training requested by Agent and all travel expenses plus per diem for the Kallo representative instructing the program will be due and payable to Kallo on or before execution of this agreement. Certification is not mandatory for Agent to become certified to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products licensed under this agreement and to benefit from commissions allocated to Agent as outlined in Schedule A of this agreement. Non-compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will automatically void this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Certification *is* mandatory, which aligns with the Agent potentially having to pay for training. However, the changed text explicitly states that Certification *is not* mandatory, while simultaneously retaining the clause that non-compliance with Section 2(c) voids the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction because the Contradicted Text says 'Certification is not mandatory', which logically implies that the Agent does not *need* to take the training program and spend the $10,000. However, Section 2(c) automatically voids the agreement if the agent does not have certification. If the agent does not need certification, then it is illogical and contradictory that the agreement can be voided for a lack of something that the changed text says is not mandatory.

perturbation.original_text: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent shall pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 50% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 35% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 15% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.changed_text: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent is encouraged to pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 10% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 5% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 85% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stipulates the payment amount as 50%, 35% and 15% and payment is mandatory. The modified text changes payment is encouraged to 10%, 5% and 85%. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the original contract mandates payment amount to be 50%, 35% and 15%.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is not assignable by Agent without prior written consent by Kallo. In the event Agent changes its legal structure or undergoes a substantial change in ownership of its stock or other ownership interest, Kallo shall have the option of immediate termination of this Agreement. Kallo may assign this Agreement and its interest in the Kallo Mobile care suite of products to any party without the consent of Agent. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of any successor of Kallo and shall not be affected by any change in the ownership or control of Kallo. Agent shall re execute this agreement within 60 days of the effective date of the change in ownership or change in legal structure or change of name. "Substantial change" in ownership is defined as a change in at least 51% ownership of the Agent. KALLO - MOBILECARE b) Limitations: Agent shall distribute only Kallo Mobile care suite of products that Agent has obtained from Kallo and not from any other seller of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products. For Tax reasons such distribution is deemed to take place at the Agent's business location identified above. c) Pricing: The pricing given in Schedule A is the transfer price from Kallo to the Agent and the Agent shall, in its discretion establish a reasonable price for each of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products EULA as per Schedule A. For Maintenance Contract pricing and details refer to Schedule A, section (9), subparagraph (B). Kallo reserves the right to revise and republish prices as shown in Schedule A from time to time. d) Payment terms: For each Kallo Mobile care suite of products license ordered by Agent from Kallo, Agent is encouraged to pay to Kallo the transfer price set forth in Schedule A. 10% of the total amount to be paid with the signed purchase order, 5% upon shipping of the goods to the carrier at Canadian port and 85% upon completion of the installation. Upon delivery of the system the Agent is responsible for getting the signed EULA from the customer before the installation process starts.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated specific payment percentages (50%, 35%, and 15%), while the changed text states that the agent is 'encouraged' to pay different percentages (10%, 5%, and 85%). The contradicted text section also reflects the modified payment terms ('Agent is encouraged to pay... 10%, 5%, and 85%'), but the initial contract wording dictated specific payments, creating a conflict. Since the changed text modifies the original mandatory payment terms and introduces different percentages, it creates a direct conflict making enforcement unclear, thus a contradiction.
